attempts.error: `pnpm run check` failed: unused `apply`/`reviewer`/`designer` variables in adv-skill-backed-commands-assets.test.ts, `no-useless-assignment` in tools/worktree/index.ts, then Prettier format check.

subagent_reports.topic: Design validation for enforcePreviewVerification preview-proof strengthening

sources.label: Existing contract rq-acceptancePreviewUrl01 (spec.json)
sources.summary: Existing MUST requirement already enforces visual_surface true/false/unknown, sanitized Preview URL, bounded reachability evidence, blocking on missing URL/evidence/drift, not_applicable for non-visual. 5 scenarios. Design D1 strengthens this in place — feasible, no new requirement needed.

sources.label: /adv-review Preview URL Proof table + rules
sources.summary: Table has live/not_applicable/blocked rows; rules cover sanitize, bare-URL-insufficient, no-HTTP-probing, drift. Strengthening to exact-route/hydration/viewport/freshness is an additive prose/table edit — structurally feasible, no code change.

sources.label: /adv-discover Preview applicability
sources.summary: Records visual_surface true|false|unknown + rationale today. D4 adds preview_expectation sub-fields (exact_route_required, data_state_expectation, viewport_expectation). Additive to existing agreement section — feasible.

sources.label: Apply/designer/reviewer packet anchors
sources.summary: VISUAL_CONTEXT already carries viewport_targets + evidence_expectation; designer/reviewer already require viewport context + fallback rationale. D5 (add exact-route/post-hydration naming) is a text refinement on existing anchors — feasible, no schema change.

sources.label: Asset test pinning the contract
sources.summary: Test substring-matches review.md including the literal `\`live\` | \`visual_surface: true\`` table-cell prefix (line 530) and only checks workflowDoc for the title 'Front-End Acceptance Preview URL', not the strengthened body. Rewriting the review table must preserve that exact substring or update the assertion in lockstep.

sources.label: Spec doc generation model
sources.summary: docs/specs/advance-workflow.md is an AUTO-GENERATED mirror produced by generateSpecDocFile at ARCHIVE time only. No standalone regen script and not regenerated by `pnpm run check`. Manual markdown edits persist until next archive regenerates from spec.json. Design D6/step 3 'update markdown manually' works but is a mirror, not source of truth.

sources.label: Structural frontend classifier
sources.summary: isStructuredFrontendScope() backs visual_surface===true as structural; filename/title regex is advisory-only. Confirms agreement constraint 'classification structural not path-heuristic-only' is already satisfied — design does not regress it.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Design is the boring, correct choice: strengthen the existing rq-acceptancePreviewUrl01 contract + command/agent prose + asset tests rather than build a browser-automation framework or new MCP tool. Every anchor the design touches exists today and the proposed edits are additive (spec body/scenarios, review table rows/rules, discover proof-expectation fields, packet text). Enforcement stays workflow-owned (/adv-review pre-acceptance + contract reviewMatrix), consistent with current architecture where preview proof is already prose+matrix-enforced, not parser-enforced. No layer-boundary, schema-registry, or worker-bundle impact. Two concrete drift hazards: (1) the asset test substring-matches the exact `\`live\` | \`visual_surface: true\`` table cell, so a table rewrite must preserve that prefix or co-update the test; (2) docs/specs/advance-workflow.md is an archive-time auto-generated mirror, so the design's 'update markdown manually' step lands edits that are not the source of truth and are NOT pinned for strengthened-body content by the current title-only doc assertion — the durable law lives in spec.json.

validation.notes: No hard conflicts. Two required corrections before/within implementation: (C1) Preserve the literal review.md substring `\`live\` | \`visual_surface: true\`` when rewriting the Preview URL Proof table, OR update the assertion at adv-skill-backed-commands-assets.test.ts:605 in the same change; otherwise the GREEN asset test breaks. (C2) Treat .adv/specs/advance-workflow/spec.json as the authoritative law for the strengthened body; the docs/specs/advance-workflow.md edit is a mirror only (archive-time generated). If the design wants the strengthened body pinned in tests, add a spec.json body assertion (e.g. expect requirement body to contain 'exact route' / '375' / 'hydration') rather than relying on the markdown mirror, since the current doc assertion only checks the title. Nonblocking: the 375px '(or project equivalent)' wording must stay parametric to honor the agreement constraint against overfitting one framework/provider.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Proceed to design gate as READY-with-corrections (CAUTION). The strengthen-in-place approach is sound, source-feasible, and architecture-aligned. Before completing planning: (1) add the test-substring-preservation note to the implementation plan for the review.md table edit, (2) anchor strengthened-body assertions on spec.json (not the generated markdown mirror), and (3) keep viewport wording parametric (375px OR documented project equivalent). No new MCP tool / browser framework is needed or justified.
follow_ups: Implementation: extend the asset test to pin strengthened spec.json body phrases (exact route, hydration/readiness, viewport/375, fixture/mock, stale/cache, URL-source-only insufficiency) AND a non-visual not_applicable assertion, per AC9/AC10.

Implementation: confirm whether any other asset test (e.g. adv-reviewer-asset.test.ts, subagent-reports-spec-assets.test.ts cited in the design Testing Plan) already references these anchors so RED assertions are added in the right file without duplication.

This catches spec-law, workflow, and worker-packet drift without adding a runtime parser when enforcement is intentionally workflow-owned.

`docs/specs/*.md` may be an archive-time generated mirror while `.adv/specs/*/spec.json` is authoritative. Asset tests for strengthened spec body should anchor on `spec.json`; markdown docs can be updated for readability but may not be regenerated by `pnpm run check`.

items.text: Visual changes cannot reach acceptance with only a bare, stale, fixture-only, or URL-assignment-only preview claim.

Acceptance proof for visual surfaces records exact route/state, verification method, hydration/readiness context, viewport coverage, timestamp/context, and fallback/blocker reason when proof is unavailable.
Non-visual/backend-only changes are not blocked by browser requirements; they still record `Preview URL: not_applicable` rationale.
Classification remains structural and evidence-backed, not path-heuristic-only.
`rq-acceptancePreviewUrl01` is strengthened so applicable visual work requires exact-route preview proof, not just generic URL reachability.

Preview proof schema/contract records at minimum: sanitized URL, route/path, state/source of fixture/live data, verification method, hydration/readiness signal or fallback rationale, reviewed timestamp/context, and viewport(s) checked.

For visual work with a runnable surface, at least one mobile viewport target of 375px width (or project-specific equivalent if documented) is required unless an explicit unavailable/fallback rationale is recorded.
Fixture/mock preview URLs are accepted only when explicitly labeled as fixture/mock evidence and not presented as user-facing live preview proof.
Stale/cached/error-page preview evidence must not pass acceptance; review docs require fresh session/cache-busting or equivalent freshness evidence when stale state is suspected.
`adv-review` acceptance proof and executive-summary instructions include exact-route/post-hydration/viewport evidence and block acceptance when required proof is missing.
`adv-discover` agreement guidance records `visual_surface` plus preview proof expectation (`exact_route_required`, viewport expectation, fixture/live expectation) when visual work is in scope.
Apply/designer/reviewer packet guidance remains aligned: designer/reviewer browser evidence must include the exact affected route/state and viewport context when runnable surfaces exist.
Tests assert command/spec/assets alignment for exact-route, hydration/readiness, viewport, stale/cache, and fixture/mock rules.
Existing non-visual preview-not-applicable behavior remains valid and tested; no browser evidence is required for non-visual changes.
Validation passes with targeted command/spec asset tests and `pnpm run check`.
Do not build a browser automation framework.
Do not require preview URLs or browser proof for non-visual/backend-only changes.
Do not use file/path heuristics as sole correctness authority for visual applicability.
Do not add brittle pixel-perfect or computed-style assertions as long-lived tests.
Do not allow arbitrary HTTP probing of untrusted URLs as a substitute for preview proof.
Keep generated acceptance artifacts projection-only; durable proof remains contract review matrix / executive summary evidence.
Implementing Playwright MCP tooling itself.
Enforcing pixel-perfect screenshot comparison.
Changing human acceptance semantics.
Creating deployment infrastructure or preview environments.
Rewriting designer report schema unless design proves a small additive field is required.

documents.proposal:
# Enforce verified preview handoffs

## Why

UI changes can pass tests while the preview URL handed to the user is dead, stale, fixture-only, or not the actual affected state after hydration. PokeEdge-Web recorded wisdom from `improveVisualCoherence`: a fixture card URL was offered before verifying the exact user-facing route, and a representative sample missed real mobile card-detail table misalignment.

## Problem Statement

ADV review/acceptance needs structural enforcement that user-facing preview handoffs for visible UI work are verified on the exact affected route/state and viewport before the user is asked to accept.

## What Changes

Add workflow/tool/checklist support so preview URLs or visual handoff claims require explicit verification evidence: actual route, environment, viewport(s), hydration/readiness signal, and absence of known failure states. The enforcement should be lightweight for non-UI work and stronger for visible UI changes.

## User Outcomes

- Users receive preview links that were actually opened and verified.
- Agents stop handing off fixture-only, stale, cached-error, or wrong-state URLs.
- Visible UI acceptance focuses on the real affected route/state, not only synthetic samples or broad smoke tests.
- Mobile viewport checks remain tied to actual affected pages when reachable.

## Scope

### In Scope

- Review/acceptance guidance and/or validator support for preview URL handoff evidence.
- A structural preview-verification record shape if current free-text evidence is insufficient.
- Detection/classification of visible UI work that requires preview verification.
- Browser-verification checklist updates for desktop and 375px mobile when visible UI changes exist.
- Tests for validator/classifier behavior and command/agent prompt packet updates.
- Documentation update capturing exact-route, post-hydration, cache-busting, and fixture-vs-live URL rules.

### Out of Scope

- Building a full browser automation framework.
- Requiring preview URLs for non-UI/backend-only changes.
- Pixel-perfect screenshot testing.
- Replacing human acceptance; this only improves evidence before the checkpoint.

### Must Not

- Must not block non-visual changes on browser evidence.
- Must not accept mocked fixture URLs as user-facing preview URLs unless the handoff explicitly says the environment is mocked.
- Must not use path/name heuristics alone as correctness authority; visible-surface classification should be backed by task metadata, changed files, or explicit evidence.
- Must not add brittle pixel/computed-style assertions as long-lived tests.

## Error Handling / Fallback

- If a real preview route is not reachable, the handoff must say so explicitly and include the failure mode; it must not present the URL as verified.
- If verification requires credentials or live data the agent lacks, the workflow should surface a clear blocker or provide a verified lower-confidence artifact labeled as such.
- If a stale failure page was opened, the workflow should require cache-busting or fresh session verification before acceptance handoff.

## Evidence

PokeEdge-Web wisdom from `improveVisualCoherence`:

- `ws-m_SO8o`: preview URL handoffs must be verified in a real browser after hydration using the exact URL the user will open; do not offer fixture UUIDs unless mocked E2E routing is active.
- `ws-OP0oDF`: visual verification must use actual affected route/state when reachable; a representative semantic-status sample missed real mobile card-detail pricing-table misalignment.

Agenda: `ag-bD1iYfbo Enforce verified preview handoffs`.

## Discovery Agenda

- Inspect current review/harden/acceptance command contracts for visual verification and preview URL language.
- Inspect existing visible-surface classifier work from `reduceAdvPromptSlop` to avoid duplicate/contradictory logic.
- Decide whether enforcement belongs in task metadata, review matrix rows, subagent report schema, validator, or command checklist.
- Define the minimal preview-verification evidence schema.
- Add regression tests for UI vs non-UI changes and mocked vs live preview handoffs.
documents.problemStatement: ADV can hand users preview URLs that were not verified on the actual affected route/state after hydration. Visible UI acceptance needs structural evidence that user-facing preview handoffs are real, reachable, and viewport-checked when applicable.
documents.agreement:
# Agreement — Enforce preview verification

## Discovery Findings

- ADV already has a preview URL acceptance contract (`rq-acceptancePreviewUrl01`) wired across spec, discovery, and review. It requires `visual_surface: true|false|unknown`, a sanitized Preview URL for visual work, reachability evidence, and review-matrix evidence before acceptance.
- Current contract still allows broad reachability evidence such as observed dev-server output, CI/deploy URL assignment, or user-confirmed URL. That proves a URL exists, but not necessarily that the agent opened the exact user-facing route/state after hydration or checked relevant viewports.
- PokeEdge-Web wisdom shows the gap: fixture/stale preview URLs and representative samples can miss actual affected route/state problems, especially mobile detail views.
- Apply/designer packets already carry `VISUAL_CONTEXT.viewport_targets` and `evidence_expectation`, and reviewer/designer agents already mention browser/viewport proof. The missing piece is a durable, structural preview proof requirement at acceptance for visual surfaces.
- Existing prep readiness has frontend applicability metadata (`metadata.frontend`, `frontend_required`, `frontend_scope`) and warnings for heuristic UI-looking tasks. This can help classification but must not be the sole authority.

## Success Criteria

- Visual changes cannot reach acceptance with only a bare, stale, fixture-only, or URL-assignment-only preview claim.
- Acceptance proof for visual surfaces records exact route/state, verification method, hydration/readiness context, viewport coverage, timestamp/context, and fallback/blocker reason when proof is unavailable.
- Non-visual/backend-only changes are not blocked by browser requirements; they still record `Preview URL: not_applicable` rationale.
- Classification remains structural and evidence-backed, not path-heuristic-only.

## Acceptance Criteria

- AC1: `rq-acceptancePreviewUrl01` is strengthened so applicable visual work requires exact-route preview proof, not just generic URL reachability.
- AC2: Preview proof schema/contract records at minimum: sanitized URL, route/path, state/source of fixture/live data, verification method, hydration/readiness signal or fallback rationale, reviewed timestamp/context, and viewport(s) checked.
- AC3: For visual work with a runnable surface, at least one mobile viewport target of 375px width (or project-specific equivalent if documented) is required unless an explicit unavailable/fallback rationale is recorded.
- AC4: Fixture/mock preview URLs are accepted only when explicitly labeled as fixture/mock evidence and not presented as user-facing live preview proof.
- AC5: Stale/cached/error-page preview evidence must not pass acceptance; review docs require fresh session/cache-busting or equivalent freshness evidence when stale state is suspected.
- AC6: `adv-review` acceptance proof and executive-summary instructions include exact-route/post-hydration/viewport evidence and block acceptance when required proof is missing.
- AC7: `adv-discover` agreement guidance records `visual_surface` plus preview proof expectation (`exact_route_required`, viewport expectation, fixture/live expectation) when visual work is in scope.
- AC8: Apply/designer/reviewer packet guidance remains aligned: designer/reviewer browser evidence must include the exact affected route/state and viewport context when runnable surfaces exist.
- AC9: Tests assert command/spec/assets alignment for exact-route, hydration/readiness, viewport, stale/cache, and fixture/mock rules.
- AC10: Existing non-visual preview-not-applicable behavior remains valid and tested; no browser evidence is required for non-visual changes.
- AC11: Validation passes with targeted command/spec asset tests and `pnpm run check`.

## Constraints

- Do not build a browser automation framework.
- Do not require preview URLs or browser proof for non-visual/backend-only changes.
- Do not use file/path heuristics as sole correctness authority for visual applicability.
- Do not add brittle pixel-perfect or computed-style assertions as long-lived tests.
- Do not allow arbitrary HTTP probing of untrusted URLs as a substitute for preview proof.
- Keep generated acceptance artifacts projection-only; durable proof remains contract review matrix / executive summary evidence.

## Out of Scope

- Implementing Playwright MCP tooling itself.
- Enforcing pixel-perfect screenshot comparison.
- Changing human acceptance semantics.
- Creating deployment infrastructure or preview environments.
- Rewriting designer report schema unless design proves a small additive field is required.

## Risks / Design Questions

- Best implementation may be spec+command/test only, not TypeScript validator code, because current preview proof is enforced by review workflow and contract matrix rather than a dedicated parser.
- If adding a typed preview-proof schema to contract rows, design must avoid overfitting to one app framework or preview provider.
- Existing broad reachability examples should remain allowed only as URL-source evidence, not sufficient proof for actual visual surface verification.
- Live tool behavior requires rebuilt/deployed plugin only if TypeScript/tool schemas change; pure command/spec/docs changes still require asset sync/deploy for future sessions.
documents.design:
# Design — Enforce preview verification

## Design Summary

Strengthen the existing preview URL acceptance contract (`rq-acceptancePreviewUrl01`) rather than introducing a new runtime browser framework. The implementation should update the authoritative spec JSON, review/discovery/apply/reviewer/designer command/agent contracts, and asset tests so visual work requires exact affected route/state preview proof with hydration/readiness, viewport, freshness, and fixture/live labeling before acceptance.

Independent validator verdict: **CAUTION / READY-with-corrections**. Required corrections C1-C2 are incorporated below.

## Key Decisions

### D1 — Strengthen existing `rq-acceptancePreviewUrl01`

Use the existing `Front-End Acceptance Preview URL` requirement as the source of truth. Update `.adv/specs/advance-workflow/spec.json` so `live` preview proof for applicable visual work includes:

- sanitized preview URL
- exact route/path opened by the user
- affected state/data source (live, fixture, mock, seeded, user-provided, or unavailable)
- verification method
- hydration/readiness signal or explicit fallback rationale
- viewport(s) checked, including 375px width or documented project equivalent when runnable
- reviewed timestamp/context
- freshness/cache context when stale/cached/error-page state is suspected

Existing broad URL-source evidence (dev-server output, CI/deploy URL assignment, user-confirmed URL) remains valid only as URL-source evidence. It is not sufficient by itself for visual-surface verification.

`docs/specs/advance-workflow.md` is an archive-time generated mirror; do not rely on it as authoritative test source. Either leave it to archive generation or update it manually for readability, but RED/GREEN body assertions must anchor on `spec.json`.

### D2 — Keep enforcement workflow-owned, not browser-framework-owned

Do not add a new browser automation framework or runtime MCP tool. Enforcement remains in `/adv-review` pre-acceptance proof and contract review matrix evidence. This keeps the change small, structurally testable, and aligned with current architecture.

### D3 — Explicit preview proof states

Revise `/adv-review` Preview URL Proof table while preserving the existing literal prefix `` `live` | `visual_surface: true` `` unless tests are deliberately co-updated. New table semantics:

- `live`: visual applicability true or visual-output implementation detected; evidence includes sanitized URL plus exact-route/state/hydration/viewport/freshness proof and matching `contract.reviewMatrix` row.
- `not_applicable`: visual_surface false and no implementation evidence of visual output; rationale recorded in `contract.reviewMatrix`.
- `blocked`: visual_surface unknown, drift, missing URL, URL-source-only evidence, missing exact-route proof, stale/error/cached preview, fixture/mock presented as live, missing hydration/readiness, missing required viewport, or missing matrix evidence.

### D4 — Discovery records proof expectations, not implementation details

Extend `/adv-discover` agreement guidance so preview applicability includes:

```yaml
visual_surface: true|false|unknown
preview_expectation:
  exact_route_required: true|false
  data_state_expectation: live|fixture_allowed|mock_allowed|not_applicable|unknown
  viewport_expectation: 375px|required_project_equivalent|not_applicable|unknown
  rationale: string
```

This is agreement-level acceptance boundary. Design/apply can refine implementation route details, but cannot silently downgrade visual proof needs.

### D5 — Align worker packets and browser-evidence instructions

Update apply/designer/reviewer instructions so browser/design proof for runnable visual surfaces must name exact affected route/state and viewport context. Designer `VISUAL_CONTEXT.evidence_expectation` should mention exact-route + post-hydration verification when expected. Reviewer browser evidence guidance should reject fixture/mock as live unless explicitly labeled.

### D6 — Tests are asset/contract tests first

Add/extend asset tests around current command/spec wiring:

- `adv-skill-backed-commands-assets.test.ts` should assert exact-route, hydration/readiness, viewport/375px, fixture/mock labeling, stale/cache/error handling, URL-source-only insufficiency, and non-visual compatibility.
- Strengthened spec-body assertions must read `.adv/specs/advance-workflow/spec.json` body/scenarios, not generated markdown.
- Existing preview URL wiring test should be extended rather than duplicated where possible.
- Preserve current review-table substring expectations unless co-updating tests in same patch.

## Implementation Strategy

1. Add RED asset assertions for exact-route/hydration/viewport/fixture/stale rules, anchored primarily on `spec.json` and relevant command/agent files.
2. Update `.adv/specs/advance-workflow/spec.json` requirement body/scenarios.
3. Update `docs/specs/advance-workflow.md` manually only if helpful for reviewed docs; do not depend on it for spec-body correctness.
4. Update `.opencode/command/adv-discover.md` preview applicability section to include proof expectation fields.
5. Update `.opencode/command/adv-review.md` Preview URL Proof table/rules/executive-summary text to require exact-route/state/hydration/viewport/freshness proof and block URL-source-only evidence while preserving test-sensitive table prefix.
6. Update `.opencode/command/adv-apply.md`, `.opencode/agents/adv-reviewer.md`, and `.opencode/agents/adv-designer.md` packet/guidance text for exact route/state + viewport proof.
7. Run targeted asset tests and `pnpm run check`.

## Testing Plan

- RED:
  - Extend `acceptance preview URL contract is wired across discovery, review, and specs` to fail until new exact-route/hydration/viewport/fixture/stale language exists.
  - Assert `spec.json` body/scenarios contain exact route/path, hydration/readiness, 375px or project equivalent, fixture/mock labels, stale/cache/error-page freshness, and URL-source-only insufficiency.
  - Assert review/discover/apply/reviewer/designer command/agent files carry the aligned evidence language.
  - Assert non-visual `Preview URL: not_applicable` remains present.
- GREEN:
  - `bin/oc-test targeted -- src/adv-skill-backed-commands-assets.test.ts src/adv-reviewer-asset.test.ts src/delegation-matrix.test.ts src/subagent-reports-spec-assets.test.ts`
  - `pnpm run check`

## Compatibility Plan

- No new MCP tool or runtime browser dependency.
- Non-visual behavior remains compatible: `Preview URL: not_applicable` with rationale can still satisfy acceptance.
- Existing broad reachability sources remain useful as URL provenance but no longer satisfy visual proof alone.
- Future self-application of command/spec changes requires deploy/sync before live workflow behavior changes in new OpenCode sessions.

## Risks

- Instruction-only enforcement can drift if tests are too weak. Mitigation: pin exact key phrases across spec, commands, and agent packets.
- Exact-route proof may be unavailable for some products. Mitigation: allow explicit unavailable/fallback rationale, but it must be recorded and may block acceptance when proof is required.
- Fixture/mock flows are valid for some E2E contexts. Mitigation: allow them only when explicitly labeled and not presented as user-facing live proof.
documents.executiveSummary:
# Executive Summary — Enforce preview verification

ADV now treats visual preview handoffs as verified evidence, not bare URL claims. The strengthened `rq-acceptancePreviewUrl01` contract requires exact affected route/state proof, hydration/readiness context, viewport evidence including 375px or documented equivalent, freshness/cache context when relevant, and fixture/mock labeling. URL-source-only evidence no longer satisfies visual acceptance.

Workflow guidance now aligns discovery, review, apply, designer, and reviewer surfaces around the same rule: non-visual work can still record `Preview URL: not_applicable`, while applicable visual work must provide exact-route/post-hydration/viewport proof or surface a blocker/fallback rationale.

Verification passed:

- Targeted asset suites: 179 tests passed.
- `pnpm --dir plugin run check`: schemas, typecheck, isolation, lockfile, lint, and format passed.
- Independent acceptance reviewer verdict: READY, with small in-scope remediation checkpointed.
- Contract review matrix: 26/26 rows passing or respected.
documents.acceptance:
# Acceptance

Reviewed at: 2026-06-27T21:27:40.429Z

## Contract Review Matrix

| ID | Kind | Requirement | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| SC1 | success_criterion | Visual changes cannot reach acceptance with only a bare, stale, fixture-only, or URL-assignment-only preview claim. | pass | Visual changes now require exact-route/state preview proof in strengthened rq-acceptancePreviewUrl01 and /adv-review rules; adv-reviewer verdict READY. |
| SC2 | success_criterion | Acceptance proof for visual surfaces records exact route/state, verification method, hydration/readiness context, viewport coverage, timestamp/context, and fallback/blocker reason when proof is unavailable. | pass | Preview proof contract requires method, hydration/readiness, viewport, timestamp/context, and fallback/blocker rationale; reviewer READY. |
| SC3 | success_criterion | Non-visual/backend-only changes are not blocked by browser requirements; they still record `Preview URL: not_applicable` rationale. | pass | Non-visual `Preview URL: not_applicable` remains asserted in plugin/src/adv-skill-backed-commands-assets.test.ts; targeted suite passed 179 tests. |
| SC4 | success_criterion | Classification remains structural and evidence-backed, not path-heuristic-only. | pass | Discovery/review/apply/designer/reviewer guidance aligns around structural visual-surface evidence; reviewer READY. |
| AC1 | acceptance_criterion | `rq-acceptancePreviewUrl01` is strengthened so applicable visual work requires exact-route preview proof, not just generic URL reachability. | pass | `.adv/specs/advance-workflow/spec.json` strengthened; asset test asserts exact route/path and URL-source-only insufficiency; targeted suite passed. |
| AC2 | acceptance_criterion | Preview proof schema/contract records at minimum: sanitized URL, route/path, state/source of fixture/live data, verification method, hydration/readiness signal or fallback rationale, reviewed timestamp/context, and viewport(s) checked. | pass | Spec/test assert sanitized URL, route/path, data source, method, hydration/readiness, timestamp/context, viewport, freshness/fallback evidence. |
| AC3 | acceptance_criterion | For visual work with a runnable surface, at least one mobile viewport target of 375px width (or project-specific equivalent if documented) is required unless an explicit unavailable/fallback rationale is recorded. | pass | Spec/test assert 375px width or documented project equivalent for runnable visual surfaces unless fallback rationale is recorded. |
| AC4 | acceptance_criterion | Fixture/mock preview URLs are accepted only when explicitly labeled as fixture/mock evidence and not presented as user-facing live preview proof. | pass | Spec/review guidance and tests require fixture/mock evidence be labeled and not presented as live user-facing proof. |
| AC5 | acceptance_criterion | Stale/cached/error-page preview evidence must not pass acceptance; review docs require fresh session/cache-busting or equivalent freshness evidence when stale state is suspected. | pass | Spec/review guidance and tests require stale/cached/error-page evidence to block until fresh/cache-busted or equivalent proof exists. |
| AC6 | acceptance_criterion | `adv-review` acceptance proof and executive-summary instructions include exact-route/post-hydration/viewport evidence and block acceptance when required proof is missing. | pass | `/adv-review` acceptance proof and executive-summary guidance updated; asset tests assert exact-route, post-hydration, viewport, and blocking language. |
| AC7 | acceptance_criterion | `adv-discover` agreement guidance records `visual_surface` plus preview proof expectation (`exact_route_required`, viewport expectation, fixture/live expectation) when visual work is in scope. | pass | `/adv-discover` now records `visual_surface` plus `preview_expectation` fields; acceptance reviewer added persisted-agreement guidance and matching test assertion. |
| AC8 | acceptance_criterion | Apply/designer/reviewer packet guidance remains aligned: designer/reviewer browser evidence must include the exact affected route/state and viewport context when runnable surfaces exist. | pass | Apply/designer/reviewer packet guidance aligned; asset tests assert exact affected route/state, post-hydration/readiness, viewport context, and fixture/mock labeling. |
| AC9 | acceptance_criterion | Tests assert command/spec/assets alignment for exact-route, hydration/readiness, viewport, stale/cache, and fixture/mock rules. | pass | Targeted asset suites passed: `bin/oc-test targeted -- src/adv-skill-backed-commands-assets.test.ts src/adv-reviewer-asset.test.ts src/delegation-matrix.test.ts src/subagent-reports-spec-assets.test.ts` (179 tests). |
| AC10 | acceptance_criterion | Existing non-visual preview-not-applicable behavior remains valid and tested; no browser evidence is required for non-visual changes. | pass | Existing non-visual `Preview URL: not_applicable` behavior remains asserted and passed in asset tests. |
| AC11 | acceptance_criterion | Validation passes with targeted command/spec asset tests and `pnpm run check`. | pass | `pnpm --dir plugin run check` passed: schemas, typecheck, isolation, lockfile, lint, format. |
| C1 | constraint | Do not build a browser automation framework. | respected | No browser automation framework was added; changes are spec/command/agent/test guidance only. |
| C2 | constraint | Do not require preview URLs or browser proof for non-visual/backend-only changes. | respected | Non-visual changes keep `Preview URL: not_applicable`; no browser proof required for backend/non-visual work. |
| C3 | constraint | Do not use file/path heuristics as sole correctness authority for visual applicability. | respected | Visual applicability guidance uses agreement/task/report evidence and blocks unknowns; no file/path heuristic is sole authority. |
| C4 | constraint | Do not add brittle pixel-perfect or computed-style assertions as long-lived tests. | respected | No pixel-perfect or computed-style assertions added; tests assert text contract alignment only. |
| C5 | constraint | Do not allow arbitrary HTTP probing of untrusted URLs as a substitute for preview proof. | respected | No arbitrary HTTP probing or URL probing implementation added; proof remains human/agent review evidence. |
| C6 | constraint | Keep generated acceptance artifacts projection-only; durable proof remains contract review matrix / executive summary evidence. | respected | Generated acceptance artifacts remain projection-only; durable proof captured in contract review matrix/executive summary evidence. |
| OOS1 | out_of_scope | Implementing Playwright MCP tooling itself. | respected | No Playwright MCP tooling implemented. |
| OOS2 | out_of_scope | Enforcing pixel-perfect screenshot comparison. | respected | No pixel-perfect screenshot comparison added. |
| OOS3 | out_of_scope | Changing human acceptance semantics. | respected | Human acceptance semantics unchanged; acceptance still requires user confirmation. |
| OOS4 | out_of_scope | Creating deployment infrastructure or preview environments. | respected | No deployment infrastructure or preview environment creation added. |
| OOS5 | out_of_scope | Rewriting designer report schema unless design proves a small additive field is required. | respected | Designer report schema unchanged; no schema rewrite needed. |
